Clothing and Personal Gear 
Citizens of Alpha Complex wear jumpsuits ‘or other garments in the Color of ther secur ty clearance. At lower clearances the garments tend to be coarse, baggy, poorly-designed and iliting. As a citizen rises in status, a good portion of his credit surplus is often Spent in Improving the quality of his clothing. The finer the clothing quality, the more important the citizen, Though all jumpsuits must match the standard design ordained by The Computer, the quality ofthe material, workmanship, and ‘ornamentation isan unmistakable measure of status in Alpha Complex Personal gears limited, since The Computer provides for everyone's needs. Most ofa low. status citizen's possessions can be carried in the utility bel thai prt of the standard jump- Suit uniform. Individual possessions are usual- ly limited to items for hygiene, a few small wworkrelated objects, and lucky. charms, mementoes, and other items of purely per sonal significance. Since there i so litle privacy, personal tems are guarded with great care. A citizen will Seldom takeoff is utility belt and leave it una tended. (Though theft is treasonous, iis sll immensely popular in Alpha Complex.) 
Everyday Life ‘Most of the citizens of Alpha Complex are either very happy or ina state hard to distinguish from happiness.
